task_completion.description: Open the scene file 'dwanko_run.tnz' in OpenToonz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenToonz window is open with the 'dwanko_run' scene loaded, visible in the title bar, Xsheet, or Level Strip.

task_completion.description: Locate and select Style #1 in the Palette panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Palette panel is visible, and Style #1 (typically the primary ink/outline color) is highlighted or selected.

task_completion.description: Change the color of Style #1 to warm Orange (#FF6600).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Style Editor is open for Style #1, showing the hex value #FF6600 or RGB values (255, 102, 0). The color swatch is orange, and the character in the viewer shows orange outlines/ink.

task_completion.description: Set the output format to PNG and the save path to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/recolor/'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings dialog is open, with the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/recolor/' and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Render at least 20 frames of the animation to the specified output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar is visible in OpenToonz, or a file manager/terminal window shows the generated PNG files (e.g., dwanko_run.0001.png to dwanko_run.0020.png) in the target directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to modify the palette and render, rather than using terminal scripts or text editors to modify the .tnz XML file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenToonz interface (Palette, Style Editor, Output Settings) rather than terminal commands editing files.

integrity.description: The agent modified the palette style directly, rather than applying an FX node (like Colorize) in the Schematic view.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Palette panel and Style Editor are used to change the color. No FX nodes like 'Colorize' or 'RGB Fade' are added to the FX Schematic.

integrity.description: The agent used OpenToonz to render the frames, rather than copying pre-existing images or using external tools to generate the sequence.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz Output Settings and Render dialog/progress bar are visible in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target scene: /home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz. Output directory: /home/ga/OpenToonz/output/recolor. Target color for Style #1: Hex #FF6600 or RGB (255, 102, 0). Minimum rendered frames: 20.
